behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at real `git` subprocess output semantics (rename detection/status codes) plus CLI parsing. A mock can hide real rename edge cases (rename score thresholds, status format variants). Integration is too low because behavior depends on real subprocess interaction. Agentic/Workflow rejected: behavior text is purely git diff categorization, no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp real git repo, commit baseline, rename files with `git mv` and with content edits, run the CLI against real repo state, assert report includes renamed count and does not increment added/deleted for those paths.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures typically appear with real git diff output and real filesystem blobs, not synthetic parser fixtures alone. Mocking git output can miss subprocess encoding/format behavior that causes crashes. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real git repo, add/modify binary files (e.g., random bytes/image), run CLI over changes, assert process exits successfully and binary file changes are represented in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git behavior for unborn `HEAD` and subprocess exit/error text. In-process fakes can miss real command failure modes and return codes. Integration is too low because the key failure is real git process state. Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior wording.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new real git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and non-error exit code.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual git history across commits/index/worktree; real subprocess and repository state are the failure boundary. Mocking history responses risks tautological tests that miss real command/query mismatches. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI decision involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies across multiple commits, run CLI for target range/state, compare reported category counts to counts independently computed from real git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets git index vs working-tree semantics, which require real git state transitions and subprocess queries. Fakes can mask mismatches in how staged/unstaged are read. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no LLM/ML dependency or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create identical logical change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI in both scenarios, assert structured stdout is identical.
